title_original: Google Online Security Blog: Cultivating a robust and efficient quantum-safe HTTPS
summary_original: Google's Chrome Secure Web and Networking Team announced a phased rollout of Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs) to enable quantum-resistant HTTPS while minimizing performance and bandwidth issues associated with post-quantum cryptography. Currently, a feasibility study with Cloudflare is underway, using MTCs alongside traditional X.509 certificates. By early 2027, Chrome will invite trusted Certificate Transparency Log operators to bootstrap public MTCs, followed by onboarding additional Certification Authorities into a new Chrome Quantum-resistant Root Store supporting only MTCs. MTCs aim to provide compact authentication, default transparency, and post-quantum security without today’s Certificate Transparency overhead. This initiative is being developed in collaboration with the Internet Engineering Task Force's PLANTS working group, emphasizing security, simplicity, and resilience for a post-quantum internet.
